units.name: Human Infantry
units.notes: Core human infantry troops. Base stats example - actual stats determined by ancestry and equipment.

units.name: Human Artillery (Longbow)
units.notes: Human archers with longbows. Squishy but dangerous from range.

units.name: Bloodfangs
units.notes: Nasty cavalry unit with good combat prowess. Quite effective in battle.

units.name: Goblin Sappers
units.notes: Specialized goblinoid sappers capable of taking out fortifications. Can potentially breach hero defenses.

units.name: Goblin Worg-riders
units.notes: Mobile goblinoid cavalry riders mounted on fierce worgs.

units.name: Bugbear Heavy Claw
units.notes: Powerful heavy assault unit. Wreaks havoc against infantry. Significant threat on battlefield.

units.name: Grim Front
units.notes: Undead special unit granted by defeating/not defeating Caliga Lifebane the necromancer. Only available if necromancer's activities not disrupted.
units.availability: Conditional - only if Caliga Lifebane not defeated
units.reference: See Domains & Intrigue chapter page 87 for full details

unitTypes.name: Infantry
unitTypes.description: The meat-and-potatoes troops for any domain. Infantry have higher Toughness than other units of the same tier and ancestry.

unitTypes.name: Artillery
unitTypes.description: Includes both archers and siege engines. Can attack any other unit on the field.
unitTypes.notes: Archers are squishy with low Defense/Toughness. Siege engines usually Tier II, require full round between attacks, can damage fortifications.

unitTypes.name: Cavalry
unitTypes.description: Move fast and hit hard. Can range across the battlefield attacking cavalry, infantry, and exposed artillery units.
unitTypes.notes: Higher Power than other units. Often one of few Tier I units dealing 2 damage per casualty.

unitTypes.name: Aerial
unitTypes.description: Flying units rare, mostly Tier III with stats comparable to average Tier II. Can attack any unit.
unitTypes.notes: Higher Attack modifiers but low Defense and Toughness. Psychological impact often greater than actual battlefield effect.

unitTypes.name: Levies
unitTypes.description: Untrained laborers and townfolk pressed into service. Cannot improve equipment or experience.
unitTypes.notes: Best used defending rear against cavalry. Cheap to field (2 levies = 1 regular Tier I unit).

unitTypes.name: Siege Engine
unitTypes.description: Usually Tier II units. Can attack any unit and can damage fortifications. Require an entire round between attacks.
unitTypes.notes: Only unit that can damage fortifications. Typically only mustered when fighting fortifications.

ancestries.name: Dwarf
ancestries.description: Sturdy, strong warriors, good with engineering

ancestries.name: Goblinoid
ancestries.description: Diverse troops including goblins, bugbears, hobgoblins

martialAdvantages.name: Skirmishers
martialAdvantages.description: If any infantry or artillery unit this commander controls moves before it attacks, it has +2 to Attack tests for that activation, and it can immediately move 1 space after it attacks.

martialAdvantages.name: Death Commandos
martialAdvantages.description: When any infantry unit this commander controls succeeds on a Power test as part of an attack, that target unit must succeed on a Morale test or suffer 1 additional casualty.

martialAdvantages.name: Patron's Curse
martialAdvantages.description: If any heavy or super-heavy unit this commander controls makes a successful Power test made as part of an attack, the target unit is disoriented until the end of its next activation.

tiers.name: Tier II
tiers.dependency: Can field one less than current number of Tier I units

tiers.name: Tier III
tiers.dependency: Can field one less than current number of Tier II units

tiers.name: Tier IV
tiers.dependency: Can field one less than current number of Tier III units

tiers.name: Tier V
tiers.dependency: Can field one less than current number of Tier IV units
